events.summary: Restarted Faith in Mind as a fresh local-only edition package.
events.details: The earlier premature OpenZen Faith in Mind scaffold was removed before production work continued.

events.summary: Locked the free witness hunt after two consecutive no-new-free recon waves.
events.details: The broader free witness search remained open until the lock rule was satisfied, then the candidate set was frozen for production work.

events.summary: Assigned and froze stable witness sigla.
events.details: Sigla T1-T5, A1-A3, C1-C17, and S1-S5 became the canonical short witness identifiers for the edition package.

events.summary: Locked T1 as the starting copy-text spine.
events.details: T1 is the starting copy-text for the critical edition, but the lock is reversible only through explicit evidence, a decision record, and a timeline event.

events.summary: Generated stable page images and page map for T1.
events.details: The canonical T1 PDF was re-verified and rendered to 83 PNG page images at 200 DPI. A page map and OCR metadata record were created.

events.summary: Installed the first OCR engine stack for the edition package.
events.details: RapidOCR, ONNX Runtime, and OpenCV were installed because the machine did not have system Tesseract or ImageMagick available.

events.summary: Completed RapidOCR pass 1 across all T1 page images.
events.details: RapidOCR processed all 83 pages, extracted text from 80, and returned no text on the final 3 pages.

events.summary: Pinned the Python 3.12 Paddle stack to a documented compatibility pair.
events.details: The package switched to paddleocr 3.2.0, paddlepaddle 3.1.1, and paddlex 3.2.1 after the newer stack failed during prediction.

events.summary: Made PaddleOCR calibration-usable on T1-p001 with PP-OCRv4.
events.details: The default PP-OCRv5 path still crashed on this machine, but PP-OCRv4 succeeded on the calibration page with CPU, no-HPI, and no-MKL-DNN settings.

events.summary: Completed the four-engine same-page calibration slice on T1-p001.
events.details: Saved comparison outputs now exist for RapidOCR, Tesseract, EasyOCR, and PaddleOCR on the same calibration page.

events.summary: Built the first normalized T1 working spine from RapidOCR outputs.
events.details: The normalized file preserves page markers and stable line identifiers without applying editorial correction.

events.summary: Completed PaddleOCR PP-OCRv4 full pass across all T1 page images.
events.details: A resumable Python 3.12 PaddleOCR PP-OCRv4 run finished successfully across all 83 T1 pages and preserved page-level outputs.

events.summary: Started Stage 2D manual correction and opened the corrected T1 working spine.
events.details: The package now includes a corrected working text, correction log, unresolved-loci register, and a first bounded set of title and lemma-line OCR certainty fixes through T1-p079.l01.

events.summary: Recovered a broad high-confidence lemma-line spine in the corrected T1 working text.
events.details: The corrected working file now carries OCR-first recovery of title lines and poem lemma lines through T1-p079.l01, while commentary prose remains largely unresolved.

events.summary: Extended OCR support for the early noisy pages before prose correction.

events.summary: Added a full Tesseract support pass across all T1 pages.
events.details: A first full-pass attempt failed because the system Tesseract install did not resolve chi_tra traineddata. The rerun pointed TESSDATA_PREFIX at the witness-local tessdata directory and completed a saved full pass across all 83 T1 pages.

events.summary: Added geometry-sorted Paddle support text for the full T1 witness.
events.details: Saved Paddle PP-OCRv4 JSON detections were re-ordered by x-center descending and y-center ascending to create a vertical-page support view across all 83 T1 pages. This derivative is support-only and exists to reduce reading-order noise before prose correction.

events.summary: Opened `T4` as the first comparison-control OCR package.

events.summary: Declared the bounded `T4` comparison-control preparation slice.

events.summary: Declared the bounded `T4` vs `T1` comparison slice.

events.summary: Built the first `T4` vs `T1` comparison table.
events.details: The first comparison table records broad `T4` support for the recovered `T1` lemma spine while making the comparison-supported open problems explicit instead of silently rewriting `T1`.

events.summary: Opened the first `T4`-supported comparison problem loci explicitly.
events.details: The unresolved register now explicitly names the `T1-p021/p022` gap, the `T1-p032` sequence problem, `T1-p047.l01`, `T1-p065.l01`, and the closing-sequence issue around `T1-p075` to `T1-p079` as comparison-supported open problems.

events.summary: Applied strict four-engine compliance before stronger scan-witness editorial use.
events.details: The active workflow now treats a new East Asian scan witness as editorially provisional until its four-engine status block is recorded. Existing `T4` comparison notes remain provisional until the missing engine statuses are logged.

events.summary: Completed full `tesseract` pass on `T4` with warnings logged.
events.details: A full `T4` Tesseract pass is now saved under `ocr/T4/ocr/tesseract-full-pass/`. The run produced `.txt` outputs for all 28 pages and also emitted six scale warnings on very small fragments, which were preserved in the run summary instead of being hidden.

events.summary: Completed `EasyOCR` calibration on `T4-p002`.
events.details: The engine itself ran on `T4-p002`, but the first save attempt failed because the raw result payload included NumPy scalar values that were not JSON-serializable. The normalized rerun completed and saved a calibration slice under `ocr/T4/ocr/easyocr-calibration/`.

events.summary: Completed full `PaddleOCR PP-OCRv4` pass on `T4` and derived usable text support.
events.details: The saved `T4` Paddle pass now covers all 28 pages under `ocr/T4/ocr/paddleocr-ppocrv4/`. As on `T1`, the raw Paddle `.txt` companions are not the reliable text layer on this machine, so recoverable text was derived from `res.rec_texts` into `extracted-text/`.

events.summary: Recorded the full `T4` four-engine status block.
events.details: The package now records `T4` as `RapidOCR` full pass, `tesseract` full pass with warnings, `PaddleOCR PP-OCRv4` full pass, and `EasyOCR` calibration-only. This satisfies the requirement to record all four engine statuses, while still keeping stronger editorial reliance provisional until `EasyOCR` is extended.

events.summary: Completed full `EasyOCR` pass on `T4`.
events.details: The full `EasyOCR` pass was resumed after an earlier timeout and now covers all 28 `T4` pages under `ocr/T4/ocr/easyocr-full-pass/`. `27` pages contain extracted text and only `T4-p028` is empty.

events.summary: Cleared `T4` out of provisional engine-status state.
events.details: With the `EasyOCR` full pass saved, `T4` now has full-pass OCR coverage from `RapidOCR`, `tesseract`, `PaddleOCR PP-OCRv4`, and `EasyOCR`. The witness is no longer provisional merely because of missing engine-status work.

events.summary: Corrected `T1-p047.l01` and `T1-p065.l01` from the fully recorded `T4` comparison witness.

events.summary: Supplied the missing line between `T1-p021.l01` and `T1-p022.l01` as stable inserted locus `T1-p021.l01a`.

events.summary: Supplied the missing closing locus after `T1-p077.l01` as `T1-p077.l01a`.

events.summary: Rolled back the false poem certainty at `T1-p075.l01` after direct image review.

events.summary: Closed the remaining `T1-p075/T1-p076` closing-band issue as omission rather than substitution.
events.details: The package now records that `T1-p075` remains commentary material while `T1-p076.l01 = 若如是必不須守` is visually secure. `T4` supports the broader transmitted close, but there is no positive `T1` evidence for restoring `若不如此必不須守` on the previous page, so the branch question is resolved as omission in this witness.

events.summary: Completed the full `EasyOCR` pass on `T5` after normalizing the serializer path.

events.summary: Adopted evaluation-driven authority, evidence-strength, and stage-separation safeguards for the package.
events.details: After reading `critical_edition_evaluation.md`, the package explicitly adopted three tightening rules: `current-state.md` is the authoritative resumability surface when package logs disagree, non-trivial editorial actions must carry explicit evidence basis plus evidence strength, and recon / transcription / collation / edition stages must not be mixed casually. The governing repo docs and this package decision layer were updated before further `T2` OCR-compliance work continued.

events.summary: Completed the full `PaddleOCR PP-OCRv4` pass on `T2` and derived the usable text layer.

events.summary: Completed the full `EasyOCR` pass on `T2` using the same logged OCR-input derivatives.
